summary: Fable design of the E2 two-Dolt-store mechanism + E1.2 CLI periphery census

e2.storeOpenWiring:
A "store" in this codebase is a SCOPE-ROOT DIRECTORY carrying `.beads/config.yaml` (issue_prefix, endpoint) + `.beads/metadata.json` (dolt_database, backend); all scopes of a city share ONE managed Dolt sql-server, each scope being a separate DATABASE (`canonicalScopeDoltDatabase`, cmd/gc/beads_provider_lifecycle.go:462 → `defaultScopeDoltDatabase`:450 returns "hq" for the city dir, else the scope prefix). So a 2nd city INFRA Dolt store is simply a second scope root — no beads-factory change needed.

CHOSEN LOCATION: `infraScopeRoot(cityPath) = filepath.Join(cityPath, ".gc", "infra")` (new helper in cmd/gc/main.go next to fileStoreLayoutMarkerPath:1114; `.gc/` is the established runtime-state dir). Its Dolt database name will be its prefix (see reservedPrefixes field: recommend "gcg"), created by `bd init` through the existing `initAndHookDir(cityPath, dir, prefix)` (beads_provider_lifecycle.go:499) — `desiredScopeDoltConfigStateForInit` (:319) already handles a non-rig dir via its fabricated-rig fallback arm (:348), so the infra scope rides the rig-shaped endpoint state (inherited-city endpoint, own database) with zero contract changes.

OPEN PATH: add to cmd/gc/main.go, mirroring openCityStoreResultAt (main.go:1108):
- `func cityHasInfraStore(cityPath string) bool` — stats `<infraScopeRoot>/.beads/config.yaml` (the boundary/presence gate; analogous to the fileStoreUsesScopedRoots marker pattern main.go:1118).
- `func openCityInfraStoreResultAt(cityPath string) (beads.StoreOpenResult, bool, error)` — returns (zero, false, nil) when cityHasInfraStore is false; else `openStoreResultAtForCity(infraScopeRoot(cityPath), cityPath)` (main.go:1183). That function already does everything right for a non-city scope: `resolveStoreScopeRoot` (:1272), provider resolution, `openBdStoreAt` → `bdStoreForRig(storePath, cityPath, cfg)` (:1295, works for any dir: `issuePrefixForScope` bd_env.go:234 reads the scope's own `.beads/config.yaml` via readScopeIssuePrefix:254 before falling back to cfg.Rigs), doltlite optimization (:1296), and — critically — `wrapStoreWithBeadPolicies(result.Store, cfg)` (:1231), which is the REAL production shape (the graph-split audit's leverage-bug lesson: never hand out an unwrapped store).
- `func openCityInfraStoreAt(cityPath string) (beads.Store, bool, error)` thin unwrap for CLI use, plus a per-process memoized variant `cachedCityInfraStore(cityPath, cfg)` (sync.Map keyed by clean cityPath, mirroring the cityDoltConfigs cache in beads_provider_lifecycle.go) so one-shot CLI commands don't re-open per resolve call.

CONTROLLER (controllerState, cmd/gc/api_state.go): add field `cityInfraStore beads.Store` + `cityInfraDiagnostic *beads.BeadsDiagnostic` next to cityBeadStore (:56). In newControllerState (:154-163), after the city store opens, call a new swappable seam `newControllerStateOpenCityInfraStore = openCityInfraStoreResultAt` (parallel to newControllerStateOpenCityStore:97 so tests can inject MemStores) and wrap with `wrapWithCachingStore(ctx, store, ep, true)` (:176) exactly like the city store — the infra store MUST get the CachingStore + event-feed treatment or reconciler reads regress to bd-subprocess-per-read. In update() (:615-672) reopen/swap/schedule-close it symmetrically with cityStore (add to the oldCityStore close block :668). Also append it to beadEventStoresLocked's fallback fan-out (:552-559) so cache events reach it.

CITYRUNTIME (cmd/gc/city_runtime.go): add `standaloneCityInfraStore beads.Store` next to standaloneCityStore (:100) and accessor `func (cr *CityRuntime) cityInfraBeadStore() beads.Store { if cr.cs != nil { return cr.cs.CityInfraBeadStore() }; return cr.standaloneCityInfraStore }` mirroring cityBeadStore() (:3076). Populate standaloneCityInfraStore at both standalone open sites (city_runtime.go:398 and :2015).

CLI ONE-SHOTS: never open the infra store eagerly; each resolve*Store call obtains it lazily via cachedCityInfraStore inside resolveClassStore's threading (see resolveClassStoreChange). nil/absent ⇒ identity, so every existing single-store city is byte-identical.
e2.newCityCreation:
Two touch points, both in the existing lifecycle (gc init itself only writes config; live `bd init` happens at gc start / rig-add):

1. gc init (cmd/gc/cmd_init.go, doInit:1280): after persistInitWorkspaceIdentity (~:1406) and alongside the existing hosted-Dolt canonical-config block (:1417-1426), create the infra scope's canonical on-disk identity: mkdir `.gc/infra` and write its canonical `.beads` scope files via the existing deferred-seed path — `seedDeferredManagedBeadsErr(cityPath, infraScopeRoot(cityPath), infraScopePrefix, "")` (beads_provider_lifecycle.go:415), the same mechanism initDirIfReady uses when Dolt isn't running yet (:253). This makes cityHasInfraStore(cityPath) true from birth for NEW cities — presence of the scope IS the activation boundary. Existing cities are untouched (no dir → identity routing).

2. gc start (startBeadsForConfiguredScopes, beads_provider_lifecycle.go:~211-227): immediately after `initAndHookDir(cityPath, cityPath, beadsPrefix)` (:216), add: `if cityHasInfraStore(cityPath) { if err := initAndHookDir(cityPath, infraScopeRoot(cityPath), infraScopePrefix); err != nil { return fmt.Errorf("init city infra beads: %w", err) } }`. initAndHookDir does the whole job: canonical scope files (:509), `bd init` creating the Dolt DATABASE named after the prefix (:512 initBeadsForDir; defaultScopeDoltDatabase:450 returns the prefix for a non-city dir), post-init SHOW DATABASES verification (:535), and hook install. The same guarded call goes into initDirIfReady's managed-dolt arm only if we want `gc rig add` to heal a missing infra store — not required for E2.

3. gc init --from-dir / doInitFromDir* (cmd_init.go:1716-1808) inherit (1) automatically since they funnel into doInit.

Existing cities: NO auto-creation. A live comingled city must not silently grow an empty second store while its old infra beads sit in the domain store (two-sources split-brain). Migration (stop city → create scope → move classified beads → start) is a separate owner-gated step outside E2; until then those cities stay identity via the absence gate.
e2.controllerThreading:
Thread the store top-down through the EXISTING seams; no new plumbing shape:

- controllerState holds `cityInfraStore` (opened+CachingStore-wrapped in newControllerState api_state.go:154, swapped in update():615). New exported accessor `CityInfraBeadStore() beads.Store` next to CityBeadStore (:1183) for CityRuntime and the api.State surface.
- The five typed accessors are the ONLY readers: SessionsBeadStore (:1211), GraphBeadStore (:1226), NudgesBeadStore (:1197) and class_store.go mailBeadStore (:43), ordersBeadStore (:67) each pass cs.cityInfraStore into their resolve*Store. Everything downstream (session reconciler snapshot via CityRuntime.sessionsBeadStore class_store.go:110 → loadSessionBeadSnapshot city_runtime.go:3100, dispatcher graph work via graphBeadStore:100, mail provider, nudge sweep, order tracking) inherits the routing with zero call-site edits — that is what E1 bought.
- CityRuntime: cityInfraBeadStore() prefers cr.cs.CityInfraBeadStore(), falls back to standaloneCityInfraStore (populated at city_runtime.go:398/:2015).
- runBeadCloseAutoclose (api_state.go:524) already resolves graph walks through cs.GraphBeadStore() (:534) — correct post-split for free.
- beadEventConfiguredStoreLocked (api_state.go:562): add a reserved-prefix arm before the configured-prefix scan — `if p, ok := prefixToClass(id); ok { return cs.cityInfraStore, true }` using config.ReservedClassPrefixes(), so gcs-/gcg-/gcm-/gco-/gcn- bead events route straight to the infra cache instead of the all-stores fallback. Same for autocloseStoreRefLocked (:500) — reserved-prefix ids get the infra store-ref (a new "city-infra" workflowStoreRef arm).
- CLI seam: cliSessionStore / cliSessionFrontDoor (cmd/gc/cli_session_store.go:20/:30) and the nudge/mail/order CLI resolve sites keep their exact signatures; they internally source the infra store from cachedCityInfraStore(cityPath, cfg). The ~40 cliSessionStore call sites (cmd_session.go, cmd_nudge.go, cmd_prime.go, city_status_snapshot.go, cmd_restart.go, cmd_handoff.go, cmd_runtime_drain.go) need NO edits.
- sessionsBeadStore()/graphBeadStore() etc. on controllerState (class_store.go:25-71) delegate to the exported accessors as today, so the CLI-vs-controller resolution stays single-sourced.
e2.invariantTest:
The completeness forcing function: after a representative run, EVERY bead in each store is classified with coordclass.Classify (internal/coordclass/classify.go:77) and any bead on the wrong side fails the test with its ID, type, labels, and class. Any create path not yet routed through the typed accessors surfaces as a named failing bead — you cannot ship E2 with an unrouted site.

BUILD IT SO IT RUNS ON THE REAL TWO-STORE SHAPE (the graph-split audit's core lesson — its 73 gaps hid because tests used the wrong store shape, never wrapStoreWithBeadPolicies over the router):

1. Shared assertion helper in cmd/gc (boundary_invariant_test.go): `assertStoreClassBoundary(t, label string, store beads.Store, wantInfra bool)` — `store.List(beads.ListQuery{IncludeClosed: true, TierMode: beads.TierBoth, AllowScan: true})`, then for each bead: `class := coordclass.Classify(b)`; infra := class != coordclass.ClassWork; if infra != wantInfra → t.Errorf with full bead identity. Domain stores assert wantInfra=false (TestNoInfraBeadInDomainStore); the infra store asserts wantInfra=true (TestNoDomainBeadInInfraStore). Also assert the ID-prefix half: every bead in the infra store carries a reserved class prefix (config.IsReservedClassPrefix on the id's prefix segment) once E2.4 lands, and no domain-store bead does.

2. Representative run, two tiers:
   a. FAST (cmd/gc unit, runs in `make test`): construct the controller through the production constructor with the injectable open seams pointed at REAL policy-wrapped stores — newControllerStateOpenCityStore (api_state.go:97) and the new newControllerStateOpenCityInfraStore return `wrapStoreWithBeadPolicies(beads.NewMemStore(), cfg)` / rig stores likewise, i.e. the SAME wrapper stack production uses minus only the Dolt transport. Then drive the production write paths: sling a formula via sling.DoSling with SlingDeps{Store: rigStore, GraphStore: cs.GraphBeadStore().Store} (materializes the molecule explosion), create a session bead via the session front door (sessionFrontDoor over cs.SessionsBeadStore()), send mail via newCityMailProvider, enqueue a nudge via enqueueQueuedNudgeWithStore, create an order-tracking bead via the order dispatch path, and create a plain task + a user convoy in the rig store. Then run both assertions over cs.BeadStores() (every rig + HQ domain) and cs.cityInfraStore.
   b. INTEGRATION (test/ with //go:build integration): `gc init` a temp city (which now creates the infra scope), `gc rig add`, gc start with the file or doltlite provider, `gc sling <rig>/agent --formula <f>`, let one reconciler tick run, gc stop; then open all stores via openStoreResultAtForCity / openCityInfraStoreResultAt (the true production open path incl. wrapStoreWithBeadPolicies + prefix resolution) and run the same two assertions. This tier is what proves the CLI one-shots (sling, nudge, mail) route correctly, because they run as real subprocess commands.

3. Make the fast test a conformance TABLE over creators, so adding a new infra-bead creator without routing it fails locally in seconds, and keep the assertion helper exported inside the package so future E-phases (Postgres backend swap, E5) rerun the identical invariant against their store shapes. The invariant is deliberately backend-blind: it reads only Classify(bead) and which store returned it — the boundary, never the backend.
e2.reservedPrefixes:
Activation has two halves — the store-level mint prefix and per-class explicit IDs:

1. INFRA SCOPE PREFIX = "gcg". bd mints ids as `<issue_prefix>-<suffix>` and GraphApplyPlan nodes carry NO explicit-ID field (internal/beads/graph_apply.go:53 — nodes have Key, not ID), so everything materialized through `bd graph apply` (the orchestration explosion — the overwhelming majority of infra beads) natively mints gcg-*, which is exactly the graph class's reserved prefix (internal/config/reserved_prefixes.go:17). Set it at init: infraScopePrefix constant = config's gcg value; defaultScopeDoltDatabase (beads_provider_lifecycle.go:450) then names the Dolt database "gcg" too. IsReservedClassPrefix validation (config.go:4872) already blocks users from squatting these prefixes — upgrade that HQ/rig-prefix warning to a hard error when cityHasInfraStore is true (ambiguity is no longer hypothetical).

2. PER-CLASS EXPLICIT IDS AT THE CREATE CHOKEPOINT: BdStore.CreateWithStorage already supports explicit ids (`--id`, internal/beads/bdstore.go:898-902), and beadPolicyStore.Create already computes the class (bead_policy_store.go:70 `coordclass.Classify(b)`). Add a `mintReservedClassIDs bool` field to beadPolicyStore, set only by a new `wrapInfraStoreWithBeadPolicies(store, cfg)` used exclusively by openCityInfraStoreResultAt. In Create: if the flag is set, b.ID is empty, and `config.ReservedClassPrefix(class)` returns ok, pre-fill `b.ID = prefix + "-" + newBeadIDSuffix()` (small crypto/rand base36 helper, ~8 chars, collision-checked by bd's unique-id constraint) before delegating. Sessions→gcs-, mail→gcm-, nudges→gcn-, orders→gco-, graph Creates (rare non-plan creates)→gcg-. NO new wrapper LAYER is introduced — beadPolicyStore is already the universal production wrapper, so the optional-capability assertions stay intact.

3. CONSUMERS OF THE PREFIX MAP: beadEventConfiguredStoreLocked + autocloseStoreRefLocked (api_state.go:562/:500) learn reserved prefixes → infra store (see controllerThreading); slingSourceStoreRootForCandidate (cmd_sling.go:539) maps a reserved-prefix bead id to the infra scope root so `gc sling <gcg-...>` resolves; `gc bd show`-style by-id lookups that scan BeadStores() need the infra store included in their candidate set.

4. DOC REGENERALIZE: reserved_prefixes.go's comments (":8 'SQLite-relocated coordination class'", ":24 same) predate the scope!=substrate principle — rewrite to "relocated coordination class (any backend): the reserved id-prefix a class's dedicated store mints", and fix the stale api_state.go:1220-1224 GraphBeadStore doc ("legacy .gc/beads.sqlite location (or the gcg Postgres schema)") to describe the city infra store. Same sweep over class_store.go's "single-store city ... identity today" comment block (:11-18) and the resolve*Store doc comments (:239-280), which all describe the pre-E2 world.
e2.e23SlingSplitBrain:
The split-brain: `gc sling` opens the store for the SOURCE bead — the RIG store when the bead/formula is rig-scoped (cmd_sling.go:310-323, openSlingStoreForSource / openStoreAtForCity(storeDir,...)) — and SlingDeps.GraphStore is never set anywhere in production (grep confirms zero `GraphStore:` assignments in cmd/gc and internal/api), so `deps.graphStore()` (internal/sling/sling.go:161-166) collapses onto deps.Store and the ENTIRE molecule explosion (root + steps + synthetic convoy + finalize sinks) is created in the rig's domain store. The controller meanwhile resolves graph → cityBeadStore. The seam already exists; it just isn't wired.

FIX (kill the sling split-brain):
1. cmd_sling.go deps construction (:388-417): after storeDir/store resolve, open the city store (`slingOpenCityStore(cityPath)`, seam already at :171) and set `GraphStore: resolveGraphStore(cityStore, cachedCityInfraStore(cityPath, cfg), cfg, cityPath, nil).Store-equivalent` — i.e. the graph-class resolution over the city store, which is the infra store when split is active and the city store otherwise. On a split city the wisp/workflow materialization then lands in the infra store; on a legacy city behavior is byte-identical (city-store graph beads — note this is ALSO a behavior change vs today's rig-store graph beads for legacy cities, so gate: when infra store absent, keep GraphStore nil to preserve the exact legacy collapse; only a split city gets the new destination).
2. Nudges from sling: cmd_sling.go:1519 enqueues via `beads.NudgesStore{Store: store}` where store may be the rig store — route through resolveNudgesStore(cityStore, infra, ...) instead (the :1406 tryNudgeStore path already prefers the city store; make the enqueue arm match).
3. coordClassStoreCandidates (cmd/gc/session_beads.go:682) — the reconciler's per-tick "city + all rigs" candidate fan-out used by the session-iteration and work arms (build_desired_state.go:1005/:1139/:4171). Split it by class: the WORK arms keep the full city+rigs fan-out; the SESSION/infra arm, when cityHasInfraStore, collapses to a single candidate {store: cs.cityInfraStore, ref: "city"} — sessions live in exactly one store, so scanning rig stores for session beads is pure cost and a boundary leak vector. Gate on split-active so legacy cities keep the historical fan-out. Same treatment for openSourceWorkflowStores (cmd_convoy_dispatch.go:1652-1664), which fans workflow-root probes across every rig store: on a split city, workflow roots are only in the infra store.
4. Verify (invariant test tier b) that after `gc sling rig/agent --formula f` on a split city, zero gc.root_bead_id-carrying beads exist in the rig store.
e2.e25BackendGatingAudit:
Audit rule: NOTHING may choose class routing (which store owns a bead) by backend kind; backend kind may only choose transport/credentials/lifecycle. Grep targets and dispositions in this worktree:

1. `grep -rn -i sqlite cmd/gc internal/ --include='*.go' | grep -v _test`:
   - cmd/gc/main.go:1192-1197 — removed-provider hard error ("sqlite","sqlite-cgo","coordstore"). KEEP (it's a tombstone, not gating).
   - cmd/gc/api_state.go:1220 — stale GraphBeadStore doc referencing ".gc/beads.sqlite / gcg Postgres schema". REWRITE (doc only).
   - internal/config/reserved_prefixes.go:8,:24 — "SQLite-relocated" doc. REWRITE to backend-neutral (E2.4).
2. `grep -rn 'Backend == "postgres"\|Backend == "doltlite"' cmd/gc`:
   - bd_env.go:364,:372,:438,:474,:638; beads_provider_lifecycle.go:1434,:1475; cmd_doctor.go:68 — all credential/env projection and lifecycle-skip (cityUsesDoltliteBeadsBackend at beads_provider_lifecycle.go:203 skips spawning managed dolt). LEGITIMATE: they choose transport, not class ownership. Confirm none is consulted by resolveClassStore or the typed accessors.
   - beads_provider_lifecycle.go:500-507 initAndHookDir's `scopeUsesPostgresBackendForInit` early-return — verify the new infra-scope init call composes with it (a postgres-backed city's infra scope must also short-circuit correctly).
3. NEW GUARD TEST (the durable teeth, modeled on frontdoor_di_guard_test.go / worker_boundary_import_test.go): TestClassStoreDispatchIsBackendBlind — read cmd/gc/class_store.go source and fail on any occurrence of `Backend`, `Provider`, `sqlite`, `postgres`, `doltlite` outside comments; the dispatch point must gate on infraStore presence + class name only. This converts the graph-split audit's leverage-bug lesson ("is sqlite" gating made 4 shipped fixes dead) into a permanent CI invariant.
4. Sweep `grep -rn 'graph_store\|GraphOnly' cmd/gc internal/beads` — only template_resolve.go:574 PackGraphOnlyDirs (unrelated packaging concept). Confirms the old graph_store=sqlite plumbing isn't in this branch; nothing to convert, only to not reintroduce.
e2.risks:
1. CAPABILITY-ASSERTION BREAKAGE (highest): any path that hands out the infra store must return the policy-wrapped(+cached) instance, never a re-wrap — GraphApplyFor/HandlesFor/StorageCreateStore assert on concrete types (class_store.go:14-18 comments). Mitigated by opening through openStoreResultAtForCity and minting inside beadPolicyStore rather than a new wrapper layer; the invariant test's sling arm exercises ApplyGraphPlan end-to-end.
2. BY-ID READS ACROSS THE BOUNDARY: code that Get()s an infra bead through a domain store (federation/by-id default root = CityBeadStore, class_store.go:77-80 comment) silently 404s post-split. beadEventConfiguredStoreLocked/autocloseStoreRefLocked/slingSourceStoreRootForCandidate get explicit reserved-prefix arms; anything missed surfaces as a stranded bead in the invariant/integration tests, but READ misses (not creates) won't — budget a targeted grep for `.Get(` sites fed by gc.root_bead_id/session-id metadata.
3. LEGACY-CITY DRIFT: the absence gate keeps existing cities byte-identical, but a half-created infra scope (dir exists, bd init failed) would activate routing against an empty store. cityHasInfraStore checks the canonical config.yaml, and initAndHookDir's SHOW DATABASES verification (:535) fails gc start loudly — acceptable; document that deleting `.gc/infra` reverts to identity ONLY if no infra beads were written yet.
4. MIXED-MODE SESSION SWEEP: collapsing coordClassStoreCandidates to infra-only changes reconciler sweep surface; a split city that somehow has stray session beads in a rig store (e.g. an old gc binary writing during upgrade) would stop seeing them. Mitigate: version-gate (`gc doctor` check) + the invariant test.
5. AGENT-FACING bd: agents in rig scopes query via bd against the rig database; infra beads (their own session/mail) become invisible to raw `bd show` in-rig. gc mail / gc session CLIs route correctly via the accessors; audit pack templates for raw-bd infra queries.
6. CACHE/EVENT FEED: forgetting to add cityInfraStore to wrapWithCachingStore + beadEventStoresLocked degrades reconciler ticks to bd-subprocess reads (the #1978 class of regressions) — covered in wiring, called out because it fails silently (slow, not wrong).
7. Dolt server load: one extra database + one extra CachingStore reconcile loop per city — small, but the maintenance loop (startMaintenanceLoop api_state.go:419) currently targets only cs.cityBeadStore; extend Deps.Store or accept unmaintained infra store short-term (flag as follow-up).
8. bd `--id` support for reserved-prefix minting assumes the installed bd honors arbitrary explicit ids against a scope with a different issue_prefix — verify against the pinned bd version early (contract test), else fall back to scope-prefix-only (all infra beads gcg-*) and defer per-class prefixes.
e2.implementationOrder:
Smallest-first, each independently green:

E2.1a (pure refactor, byte-identical): add infraStore param to resolveClassStore + the five resolve*Store (class_store.go:231-280); thread `nil` from all accessors/callers; split TestControllerStateClassAccessorsAreIdentity into the nil-pin + the (temporarily skipped) infra-routing test. All existing tests pass unchanged.

E2.1b: infraScopeRoot/cityHasInfraStore/openCityInfraStoreResultAt/cachedCityInfraStore in main.go; controllerState.cityInfraStore field + open in newControllerState + swap in update() + CityInfraBeadStore accessor + beadEventStoresLocked append; CityRuntime standalone field + cityInfraBeadStore(); wire real values into the accessors and cliSessionStore. Un-skip the infra-routing accessor test (MemStore-injected seam). Still inert for every real city (no scope dir exists anywhere).

E2.2 (write the forcing function FIRST, TDD): the fast boundary-invariant test (TestNoInfraBeadInDomainStore/TestNoDomainBeadInInfraStore over production-shaped stores + creator conformance table). It fails on the not-yet-routed sling/nudge arms — that failing list IS the E2.3 worklist.

E2.3: SlingDeps.GraphStore wiring in cmd_sling.go (+ the :1519 nudge enqueue), coordClassStoreCandidates infra-arm collapse (session_beads.go:682 + the 3 build_desired_state.go call sites), openSourceWorkflowStores collapse — all gated on cityHasInfraStore. Fast invariant test goes green.

E2.4: gc init infra-scope seed (cmd_init.go ~:1417) + gc start initAndHookDir arm (beads_provider_lifecycle.go:216) + infraScopePrefix="gcg"; beadPolicyStore mintReservedClassIDs + wrapInfraStoreWithBeadPolicies + newBeadIDSuffix; reserved-prefix arms in beadEventConfiguredStoreLocked/autocloseStoreRefLocked/slingSourceStoreRootForCandidate; validation warning→error on split cities; doc regeneralize (reserved_prefixes.go, api_state.go:1220, class_store.go headers).

E2.5: integration-tier invariant test (real gc init/start/sling on temp city, //go:build integration) + TestClassStoreDispatchIsBackendBlind guard + the backend-gating grep audit dispositions.

E2.6 (cleanup): maintenance-loop coverage decision for the infra store; gc doctor check for half-created scopes; TESTING.md/architecture-doc updates.

Quality gates per phase: make test, go vet ./..., and for E2.4+ the integration shard (make test-integration-shards-parallel). Phases ≤5 files each; E2.1b and E2.4 are the widest and may split again along controller/CLI lines.
e2.openQuestions:
1. Infra scope location `.gc/infra` vs a top-level `infra/` dir — `.gc/` is runtime state and gitignored (matches "infrastructure, not user work"), but owner should confirm nothing (backup tooling, `gc doctor` scans, the maintenance loop) treats `.gc/` as disposable.
2. Infra scope issue_prefix: recommendation is "gcg" (bd graph apply cannot mint per-node prefixes, and graph dominates volume), with the other four classes minted via explicit `--id`. Alternative: a sixth neutral prefix (e.g. "gci") for the scope with ALL creates explicit-id — cleaner taxonomy, more moving parts. Owner call; also verify the pinned bd version accepts `--id` with a prefix differing from the scope's issue_prefix.
3. Legacy-city migration (comingled → split) is explicitly out of E2 — confirm it's E3/owner-gated and whether E2 should ship the `gc doctor` advisory ("this city predates the infra store; run the migration to activate") now.
4. Should ordersBeadStore's per-order federated scope resolution (resolveOrderStoreTarget in order_store.go/order_dispatch.go, referenced at class_store.go:64-66) also collapse to infra-only on split cities, or do rig-routed orders keep per-rig tracking beads? The classifier says order-tracking is infra (ClassOrders), so tracking beads should collapse — but the dispatch/sweep gate-read fan-out needs its own pass; flag for the E2.3 review.
5. Legacy cities + sling GraphStore: proposal keeps GraphStore nil (rig-store collapse preserved) when no infra store exists, even though the controller resolves graph→city store — i.e. the pre-existing rig-vs-city graph split-brain on legacy cities is NOT fixed by E2, only by migration. Confirm that's acceptable (fixing it for legacy cities would move new molecules to the city store mid-life — a behavior change without a boundary to justify it).
6. Whether the API layer (internal/api session/mail handlers reaching stores via api.State) has any store access NOT going through the typed accessors — E1 claims routed; the invariant test will verify, but an explicit grep pass over internal/api for CityBeadStore() uses that touch infra classes is cheap insurance.

sites.current: store, bead, storePath, err := findBeadAcrossStores(cityPath, beadID, stderr) — control bead (graph-class) processed on the raw store where it was found (city or rig, via openStoreAtForCity/openControlStoreAtForCity)
sites.routed: store = cliGraphStore(store, cfg, cityPath) after the find (cfg loaded best-effort), then runControlDispatcherWithStore as today; identity today because resolveGraphStore returns the input store verbatim

sites.current: store, err := openControlStoreAtForCity(storePath, cityPath, cfg) — raw control store; feeds dispatch.ProcessControl(store, bead, opts) at :249 (step creation via ApplyGraphPlan, root/step walks) — this is the `gc convoy control` / serve-loop root
sites.routed: store = cliGraphStore(openControlStoreAtForCity(...), cfg, cityPath); ProcessControl at :249 then inherits the routed store. MUST return the same instance (identity) or the GraphApplyStore/HandlesFor optional-capability assertions inside dispatch silently degrade

sites.current: openConvoyStores(cfg, cityPath, workflowID, func(dir){ return openControlStoreAtForCity(dir, cityPath, cfg) }) then findWorkflowBeads(info.store, workflowID) (:895) — gc.workflow_id + gc.root_bead_id membership reads and CloseAll/delete on each raw city/rig store
sites.routed: keep the federated enumeration (by-id sweep exception) but wrap each view: findWorkflowBeads(cliGraphStore(info.store, cfg, cityPath), ...) and run closeWorkflowMatches/deleteWorkflowMatches on the routed store; byte-identical today

sites.current: openStoreAtForCity(cityPath|rigPath, cityPath) raw at :1074/:1084/:1096 — backs cmdWorkflowDeleteSource/cmdWorkflowReopenSource root scans: sourceworkflow.ListLiveRoots(info.store,...) :1499 and sourceWorkflowChildSources :1517 walk workflow roots (graph-class) on the raw store

e12.plan:
E1.2 CLI periphery routing for graph/orders/nudges, mirroring the E1.1 cliSessionStore pattern (cmd/gc/cli_session_store.go) and validated against the controller's routing (CityRuntime.graphBeadStore/ordersBeadStore/nudgesBeadStore in cmd/gc/class_store.go:100-142 and controllerState.GraphBeadStore/NudgesBeadStore in cmd/gc/api_state.go:1197-1230, which all route through resolveGraphStore/resolveOrderStore/resolveNudgesStore with cfg+recorder; the controller's runBeadCloseAutoclose api_state.go:531-538 already passes GraphBeadStore().Store into the SAME doWispAutocloseWith/doMoleculeAutocloseWith cores the CLI roots leave unrouted).

Phase 1 — seam helpers (1 new file, byte-identical): add cmd/gc/cli_class_store.go with cliGraphStore(store, cfg, cityPath) { return resolveGraphStore(store, cfg, cityPath, nil) }, cliOrderStore(...) and (optionally) typed variants returning beads.GraphStore/beads.OrdersStore, exactly like cliSessionStore (nil recorder: one-shot CLI has no live bus — documented follow-up). Decide the nil-cfg policy here once: either every root loads cfg best-effort before routing, or resolveClassStore resolves class config from cityPath so nil-cfg cannot silently fall back post-split.

Phase 2 — graph roots (cmd_convoy_dispatch.go, molecule_autoclose.go, wisp_autoclose.go): route the two control-dispatcher roots (:130, :155), wrap the workflow delete/delete-source/reopen-source store views for the root/membership reads, and pass cliGraphStore(store, cfg, cityPath) as the already-plumbed graphStoreOpt in the four autoclose call sites (molecule :80/:88, wisp :55/:63) — the variadic parameter and controller precedent make these the cheapest, highest-value wins.

Phase 3 — graph creates outside the dispatcher (cmd_formula.go, cmd_order.go): gc formula cook attach path (:643→:699/:711) and gc order run wisp instantiate (cmd_order.go:692-742) get a routed graphStore for recipe/Instantiate/root-update while source-bead and tracking ops stay on their native stores.

Phase 4 — orders openers (order_store.go): thread resolveOrderStore into openCityOrderStore :83, openOrderStoreForOrder :98, cachedOrderStoresResolver :528/:534, cachedOrderHistoryStoresResolver :628/:635, and the sweep's scoped-store construction :576 (wrap inner store before embedding to preserve the structural key/label assertions).

Phase 5 — nudges cfg fix (nudge_beads.go:42) + guard test: extend the existing frontdoor_di_guard_test.go pattern (TestSessionRelocationRootsRouteThroughSessionClassStore) with graph/orders/nudges relocation-root guards that forbid the unrouted spellings at these roots. Every phase is byte-identical today because resolveClassStore (cmd/gc/class_store.go:231) is the identity resolver; verification = build + make test + the new guards pinning the routed spellings.
e12.risks: 1) Optional-capability erosion is the top mechanical risk: molecule.Instantiate silently falls back to sequential creation if the store it receives hides GraphApplyStore (cmd_order.go:688-691 documents this trap), and the order sweep's orderTrackingSweepStoreKey/Label structural assertions do not promote through wrappers — every routed form must return the SAME underlying instance (identity resolveClassStore guarantees this today; the E2 dispatch must preserve it per class, never re-wrap). 2) nil-cfg silent fallback is the graph-split-audit leverage-bug class reborn: openNudgeBeadStore already passes cfg=nil, and the autoclose/hook paths load cfg only best-effort — when resolveClassStore grows real dispatch, any nil-cfg site quietly stays on the work store and every relocation fix downstream of it goes dead; fix the policy in Phase 1, not per-site. 3) Routing the processing store does not relocate DISCOVERY: findBeadAcrossStores, the serve loop's bd work_query env (dispatch_runtime.go:476/:866), and hook-claim continuation env are by-id/env federated — at E2 flip these must point at the infra store or the control dispatcher reports pending=false while ready control beads sit invisible (the assignedWorkBeads:0 wedge class). 4) Order scope semantics shift at E2: openOrderStoreForOrder/resolveOrderStoreTarget deliberately open RIG stores for rig-scoped orders; once resolveOrderStore routes bookkeeping to the city infra store, orderStoreTargetKey dedupe, legacyOrderCityFallbackNeeded, and cooldown/last-run history continuity across the cutover need explicit migration thought — and the controller's own order dispatcher (order_dispatch.go:410/:492-499 openStoreAtForCity per target, dispatchWisp :1351) has the same unrouted shape, so CLI and controller must convert in lockstep or `gc order run` and dispatcher-driven runs will disagree on where tracking/wisps live. 5) The autoclose/hook commands are best-effort and swallow all errors (#3411 precedent): a post-split misroute there would silently stop closing molecule roots — add teeth tests (assert the graph store is consulted) rather than relying on green best-effort paths. 6) cmd_graph.go (`gc graph`) was censused and deliberately excluded: it renders dependency graphs of arbitrary/work beads, not graph-class access.
